result.stderr:
enum-generator - 5.0.1274.0
USAGE:
Generate a enum:
  enum-generator --input Path/To/Example.json --output Path/To/Enum.g.cs

  -i, --input         Required. Json file to generate the enum from.

  -o, --output        Required. Target path for the generated enum.

  -t, --type          (Default: CSharp) Type of output file to generate. Valid values: CSharp, Cil,
                      ClassLibrary, FSharp, VisualBasic

  -c, --collection    (Default: [*]) JPath to the collection to base the enum on.

  -e, --entryname     (Default: name) JPath to the name field of a entry in the input file.

  --entryvalue        JPath to a value field of a entry in the input file.

  --entrycomment      JPath to a comment field of a entry in the input file.

  --comment           Comment to add to the generated enum.

  --namespace         Namespace to add the created enum to.

  --headermode        (Default: Default) Mode to use when adding a header. Valid values: Default, 
                      None

  --indentmode        (Default: Spaces) Indentation to use when writing output text. Valid values: 
                      Spaces, Tabs

  --indentsize        (Default: 4) When indenting with spaces this controls how many.

  --newlinemode       (Default: Unix) Which newline mode to use when writing output text. Valid 
                      values: Unix, Windows

  --storagetype       (Default: Implicit) Underlying storage type for the exported enum. Valid 
                      values: Implicit, Unsigned8Bit, Signed8Bit, Signed16Bit, Unsigned16Bit, 
                      Signed32Bit, Unsigned32Bit, Signed64Bit, Unsigned64Bit

  --curlybracket      (Default: NewLine) Which curlybracket-mode to use. Valid values: NewLine, 
                      SameLine

  -v, --verbose       Enable verbose output.
